name: Membrane-Assisted Phase-Change Heat Sink
description: A new cooling paradigm for data centers and high-power electronics: a porous membrane positioned above the heater surface stabilizes two-phase flow, delivering heat fluxes up to 2 kW/cm² and a 10x reduction in thermal resistance over state-of-the-art heat sinks. As a postdoc, I led a team of six advancing this technology from TRL 3 to TRL 6.

name: Physics of Critical Heat Flux
description: My PhD thesis tackled one of the oldest unsolved problems in thermal science: why does a boiling surface suddenly fail to remove heat? By resolving the forces acting on a departing bubble, we showed the evaporation momentum limit is 3–4x higher than previously believed, achieved a 6x increase in critical heat flux through flow stabilization, and developed force-balance correlations that explain the physics rather than just fit it.

papers.label: Int. J. Heat & Mass Transfer 2023 (open manuscript)

name: Bursting of Underwater Oil Drops
description: What happens when an oil drop rises to a water-air interface and ruptures? We uncovered the mechanisms that decide whether the drop spreads, fragments, or stays intact — physics that governs oil spill cleanup and aerosol generation at the ocean surface. Published in Physical Review Letters.

name: Drop Impact on Sublimating Surfaces
description: Drops impacting dry ice freeze from below as they spread. We showed that the solidified layer thickness — set by impact velocity — controls whether a drop rebounds or fragments, with solidification delaying fragmentation and suppressing rebound. Relevant to anti-icing and self-cleaning surface design. Published in Physical Review Fluids.

name: Drop Coalescence Simulation
description: Axisymmetric volume-of-fluid simulation of a water drop coalescing into a flat pool, built on the Basilisk CFD framework with adaptive mesh refinement. Resolves spreading behavior and tracks the full energy budget through coalescence.

name: Coalescence & Spreading of Oil Drops on Water
description: My MS research: a systematic study of 11 oils deposited on water, showing that coalescence outcomes are set by the drop's Ohnesorge number and that early-time spreading is oscillatory. The scaling laws we derived matched experiments with R² = 0.99 — with direct implications for oil spill mitigation. Published in the Journal of Colloid & Interface Science.
